description: Key code requirements from ASME, AWS, API, and OSHA that field workers need to know for pipe fitting and structural steel work

ASME_B31_3_process_piping.scope: Piping in petroleum refineries, chemical plants, pharmaceutical, textile, paper, semiconductor, cryogenic — the most common code in refinery/plant construction

materials.traceability: All pipe, fittings, flanges, and bolting must have material traceability (heat number traceable to MTR/CMTR)
materials.PMI: Positive Material Identification required for alloy materials (chrome-moly, stainless, nickel alloys). Random PMI on carbon steel per project spec.
materials.storage: Stainless steel must be stored separately from carbon steel. No carbon steel tools on stainless (contamination causes corrosion).
materials.low_temp_service: Materials below -20F require impact testing (Charpy). Common: A333 Gr 6 pipe, A350 LF2 flanges/fittings.

fabrication.welding: All welding per qualified WPS (Welding Procedure Specification) with qualified welders per ASME Section IX

fit_up_tolerance.internal_misalignment: Max 1/16 inch (1.6mm) for butt welds unless WPS allows more
fit_up_tolerance.root_opening: Per WPS, typically 1/16 to 3/32 inch for GTAW root, 3/32 to 1/8 inch for SMAW root
fit_up_tolerance.out_of_round: Pipe ends must not be out of round more than 1% of OD

fabrication.tack_welds: Must be made by qualified welders using qualified WPS. Tacks incorporated into final weld must be ground to feather edge and inspected for defects.

preheat.P1_carbon_steel: Preheat to 50F minimum when ambient below 32F. Preheat to 200F for wall thickness over 1 inch.
preheat.P3_low_alloy: Preheat 250F minimum for 1/2 to 1 inch wall, 300F for over 1 inch
preheat.P4_chrome_moly: Preheat 300F minimum (1-1/4 Cr), 400F for 2-1/4 Cr and above
preheat.P8_stainless: Generally no preheat required. Maximum interpass temperature 350F.

PWHT.definition: Post Weld Heat Treatment — stress relieving the weld
PWHT.P1_carbon_steel: Required when wall thickness exceeds 3/4 inch (19mm). Typical: 1100-1200F for 1 hr per inch.
PWHT.heating_rate: Max 600F/hr divided by max thickness in inches (not to exceed 600F/hr)
PWHT.cooling_rate: Max 600F/hr divided by max thickness in inches down to 600F, then cool in still air
PWHT.band_width: Heated band minimum 3x wall thickness on each side of weld

examination.NDE_requirements: Per engineering design — fluid service category and piping class determine NDE extent
examination.category_M: Lethal service (H2S, HF, phosgene, chlorine) — 100% RT
examination.high_pressure: Chapter IX high pressure — 100% RT plus 100% UT
examination.visual_examination: Required on ALL welds — 100%, no exceptions

hydrostatic.test_pressure: 1.5 x design pressure x (stress ratio at test temp / stress ratio at design temp). For most cases: 1.5 x design pressure.
hydrostatic.temperature: Test medium temperature must be between 60F and 120F unless freeze protection added
hydrostatic.documentation: Record test pressure, hold time, ambient and fluid temperature, system description, witness signatures

pneumatic.when_used: Only when hydro is impractical (cannot get water out, freeze risk, weight concern, catalyst contamination)
pneumatic.special_requirements: Requires documented engineering approval. Increased exclusion zone. Incremental pressure-up procedure.
pneumatic.pressure_up_sequence: 25 psi initial check, then 50% test pressure hold and inspect, then increments of 10% to full test pressure

leak_test_alternative.method: Sensitive leak test (bubble test, mass spectrometer, halide detector)
leak_test_alternative.when_used: Sometimes used in lieu of full pneumatic test when approved by engineer

ASME_B31_1_power_piping.scope: Piping in power plants — boiler external piping, steam, feedwater, condensate. Key differences from B31.3.

key_differences_from_B31_3.NDE: Generally more NDE required than B31.3 normal service. 100% RT on all pressure welds in many cases.
key_differences_from_B31_3.preheat: Slightly different preheat requirements — generally more conservative
key_differences_from_B31_3.PWHT: Required at lower thickness thresholds in some cases
key_differences_from_B31_3.hydro_test: 1.5 x design pressure (same as B31.3) but minimum hold time is longer in some applications
key_differences_from_B31_3.materials: Stricter material requirements for high-temperature steam service
key_differences_from_B31_3.socket_welds: Minimum 2-pass on socket welds (B31.3 allows single pass in some cases)

field_notes.boiler_external_piping: Highest quality requirements — often 100% RT, 100% PMI, PWHT on everything
field_notes.spring_hangers: Must be set to cold position during installation, released to hot position during startup
field_notes.high_temp_materials: P91 (9Cr-1Mo-V) common in modern power plants — VERY strict welding, preheat (400F min), PWHT (1375-1425F), and hardness requirements

ASME_Section_IX.scope: Welding and brazing qualifications — the bible for welding procedure and welder qualification

WPS.definition: Welding Procedure Specification — the recipe for making the weld
WPS.essential_variables: If an essential variable changes beyond its limits, the WPS must be requalified
key_essential_variables: PWHT (adding or deleting is essential variable)

PQR.definition: Procedure Qualification Record — the test results proving the WPS works
PQR.testing: Tensile tests, bend tests (side, face, root), impact tests if required, hardness if required
PQR.must_be_welded: A PQR must be an actual test weld that was destructively tested

welder_qualification.test_types: Groove weld test qualifies for groove and fillet. Fillet weld test qualifies for fillet only.

positions.1G_1F: Flat — pipe rotated or plate flat
positions.2G_2F: Horizontal — pipe vertical axis, weld horizontal
positions.5G: Pipe fixed horizontal — welder moves around pipe (qualifies 1G through 4G on pipe)
positions.6G: Pipe fixed at 45 degrees — THE MONEY TEST — qualifies ALL positions
positions.6GR: 6G with restriction ring — qualifies for tight-access pipe welding

essential_variables_welder: Backing (with or without backing — open root is harder)
Pipe diameter range (small pipe is harder — under 2-7/8 inch OD separate qualification)
welder_qualification.expiration: Welder qualification does not expire as long as welder uses the process within any 6-month period. If 6+ months without welding that process, requalification required.
welder_qualification.continuity_log: Employer must maintain welder continuity records showing process use within each 6-month period

P_numbers_common.P1: Carbon steel (A106, A53, A105, A234 WPB)
P_numbers_common.P3: Low alloy steel, 1/2% - 2% total alloy (A335 P1)

F_numbers_common.F1: E6010 (SMAW cellulosic rod — root pass rod)
F_numbers_common.F4: E7018 (SMAW low-hydrogen rod — fill and cap)

AWS_D1_1_structural_welding.scope: Structural steel welding — applies to buildings, bridges, and other structures. Used for pipe supports, platforms, structural steel erection.

key_requirements.prequalified_joints: AWS D1.1 has prequalified joint details — if you match the detail exactly, no PQR testing needed (unlike ASME IX which always requires PQR)
key_requirements.welder_qualification: Welders must be qualified per AWS D1.1 — separate from ASME IX qualification

inspection.NDT: Per engineer of record and project specification
inspection.fillet_weld_size: Must meet minimum specified size — use fillet weld gauge
inspection.undercut: Max 1/32 inch for primary members, 1/16 inch for secondary
inspection.porosity: Max 3/8 inch diameter, sum not to exceed 3/4 inch in 12 inches

preheat_AWS.A36_A992_under_3_4_inch: 32F minimum (no preheat above freezing) for SMAW with low-hydrogen

key_requirements.weld_symbols: All structural welds must conform to weld symbols on drawings per AWS A2.4
key_requirements.backing_bars: Commonly used in structural CJP (Complete Joint Penetration) welds. Must be continuous or spliced with full penetration weld.
key_requirements.run_off_tabs: Required at ends of CJP groove welds. Remove and grind smooth after welding.

API_1104_pipeline_welding.scope: Welding of pipelines and related facilities — cross-country pipelines, tank farm piping, pipeline stations

key_differences.vs_ASME_IX: API 1104 has its OWN welder qualification rules separate from ASME IX. Welders qualified to ASME IX are NOT automatically qualified to API 1104.
key_differences.qualification_test: Typically 6G fixed position butt weld on pipe. Destructive testing: nick break, root and face bend, tensile.
key_differences.essential_variables: Different from ASME IX — change in process, wall thickness group, direction of welding (uphill vs downhill), change in joint design
key_differences.acceptance_criteria: Different from ASME B31.3 — pipeline has its own acceptance criteria for defects
key_differences.repair_limits: Strict limits on number of repairs allowed on pipeline welds

field_notes.downhill_welding: Pipeline tradition uses downhill progression with cellulosic rods (6010 root, 8010 hot pass and fill). Faster than uphill but requires different technique.
field_notes.uphill_welding: Used for heavier wall pipe and when required by procedure. Low-hydrogen rods (7018) or GTAW root with SMAW fill.
field_notes.automatic_welding: Increasingly common — orbital GMAW or FCAW with bug machines. Separate qualification requirements.
field_notes.coating: Pipeline coating must be repaired/applied at field joints after welding and NDE acceptance

scaffolding_1926_subpart_L.guardrails: Top rail at 42 inches +/- 3 inches, mid rail, and toeboard
scaffolding_1926_subpart_L.access: Ladder, stair, or ramp access — no climbing on cross-braces
scaffolding_1926_subpart_L.inspection: Competent person must inspect before each shift and after any event that could affect integrity
scaffolding_1926_subpart_L.tag_system: Green tag = safe to use, Red tag = do not use, Yellow tag = use with restrictions
scaffolding_1926_subpart_L.erection: Only by trained, competent scaffold erectors. Fall protection required during erection/dismantling.
scaffolding_1926_subpart_L.base_plates: Required on all legs. Mudsills on soft ground. Must be level.
scaffolding_1926_subpart_L.planking: Scaffold grade planks or manufactured decking. No gaps >1 inch. Overlap minimum 12 inches. Extend 6-12 inches past support.

methods: Warning line systems (only on roofs with monitor)
fall_protection_1926_subpart_M.harness_inspection: Before each use: check stitching, D-rings, buckles, labels, no burns/cuts/chemical damage
fall_protection_1926_subpart_M.anchor_points: Must support 5,000 lbs per worker OR be designed by qualified person for 2x arrest force
fall_protection_1926_subpart_M.fall_distance: Calculate before use: lanyard length + deceleration distance + harness stretch + worker height + safety factor. Minimum clearance below worker: ~18.5 ft for 6 ft lanyard with shock absorber.
fall_protection_1926_subpart_M.rescue_plan: Must have rescue plan before any work at height. Self-rescue or prompt rescue required (suspension trauma can be fatal within 30 minutes).

crane_requirements_1926_subpart_CC.operator_certification: Operators must be certified by accredited organization (NCCCO, CIC, etc.)
crane_requirements_1926_subpart_CC.daily_inspection: Operator performs pre-shift inspection per manufacturer requirements
crane_requirements_1926_subpart_CC.annual_inspection: Comprehensive inspection by qualified person — documented
crane_requirements_1926_subpart_CC.load_charts: Must be in cab and accessible. Operator must use load chart for EVERY lift.

power_line_clearance.up_to_350kV: Minimum 10 ft clearance for boom, load, and rigging

crane_requirements_1926_subpart_CC.signal_person: Required when operator cannot see load landing area or load travel path. Must be qualified.
crane_requirements_1926_subpart_CC.critical_lifts: Lifts exceeding 75% of chart capacity, multi-crane lifts, lifts over occupied buildings, blind lifts — require written lift plan reviewed by competent person.
